_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L2 'Outfitted Levy' composited keeper and produce the L3 'Veteran Levy' variant (Tier 1 top).
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Veteran Levy beat = 4 visible adds (single steel pauldron cap on anatomical-RIGHT mace-arm only, leather grip cord wrapping upper third of mace haft, four iron strap-bands radiating from shield boss in cross pattern, knife sheath on belt at anatomical-LEFT hip opposite the L2 pouch). Within-tier chained: image_urls[0] = L2 composite, image_urls[1] = L1_Base scale anchor (content not applied). Includes CRITICAL ARM PRESERVATION lead block (per Archer L3 lesson) verifying that L2 sleeves + L2 hip pouch + L2 belt + L2 rim rivets are all preserved from the input — model has tendency to revert features the previous level introduced when chaining.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — REMOVED knife-sheath positive add per AD 'remove the dagger entirely and keep the pouch'. ADDED steel-skullcap-under-coif as the new 4th add per AD 'introduce the helmet much earlier, maybe starting around level 3'. L2 hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT remains as the only belt accessory. Negatives updated: lock out knife/dagger sheath and any second belt accessory; remove the old 'no helm at L3' clauses (we now ADD a steel skullcap).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): updated downstream preserve/replace references for L2 rivets — 8-ring → 4 cardinal-point per AD detail-economy.

v5 (2026-05-07): scrub all L2 rim-rivet PRESERVE references — the L2 IRON RIM RIVETS positive add was dropped entirely (was bleeding through to other surfaces); shield stays rivetless at L3 until the L4 continuous iron rim band arrives.
v5 (2026-05-07): scrub stale L2 rim-rivet PRESERVE/negative refs — L2 IRON RIM RIVETS positive add was dropped; downstream shield-rim language no longer references L2 rivets.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

adds.title: STEEL SKULLCAP UNDER THE MAIL COIF
adds.summary: add a low-domed cool silver-gray polished steel skullcap covering the top of the head, sitting UNDER the existing mail coif drape.

adds.title: SINGLE STEEL PAULDRON CAP ON THE ANATOMICAL-RIGHT SHOULDER
adds.summary: add a small rounded steel cap covering only the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder (the mace-arm side, viewer-LEFT side of frame).

adds.title: LEATHER GRIP CORD WRAPPING THE UPPER THIRD OF THE MACE HAFT
adds.summary: add a diagonal warm-brown leather grip cord wrapping the upper third of the wooden mace haft (the section just below the bulbous mace head where the hand currently grips).

adds.title: FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS RADIATING FROM THE SHIELD BOSS
adds.summary: add four short flat iron strap-bands on the wooden face of the round shield, each band radiating outward from the central steel boss to the rim like the four arms of a cross (one band points up, one down, one to the viewer-left, one to the viewer-right).

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L2/composite/infantry_L2_v4_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Outfitted Levy' kit on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every element of the existing kit exactly as shown in the input image: (a) QUILTED BROWN LEATHER SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms — matte warm-brown leather with diagonal cross-stitching pattern, covering shoulder to mid-bicep, with a roughly 1-inch gap of bare warm peachy-tan skin visible between the lower edge of each sleeve and the upper edge of each leather forearm bracer; (b) WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with a square dark-iron buckle centered at the front and visible edge stitching — the belt is NOT a rope cord, NOT a thin strap; (c) SMALL BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap closure and small brass stud, hanging from the leather belt on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT side) — exactly ONE pouch on that side. DO NOT revert any of these features back to the existing state. DO NOT remove the leather sleeves and re-expose bare biceps. DO NOT replace the leather belt with a rope cord. DO NOT remove the hip pouch. EDIT GOAL — Apply FOUR visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, all building on the existing kit. The four upgrades together represent a 'Veteran Levy' rank step — the conscript footman has earned his veteran-levy kit. (1) STEEL SKULLCAP UNDER THE MAIL COIF (FIRST METAL HEADPIECE) — add a low-domed cool silver-gray polished steel skullcap covering the top of the head, sitting UNDER the existing mail coif drape. The mail coif still wraps the head, scalp, ears, and back-of-neck exactly as in the input; the skullcap is visible as (a) a small smooth cool-gray steel dome at the very top of the head where the coif's scale mesh thins out at the apex, AND (b) a thin steel rim band visible at the brow line just above the eyebrows, wrapping forward across the forehead inside the coif's facial opening (~1 inch tall steel rim band, distinct from leather, distinctly metal). Two small dark-iron rivets at the temples where the steel cap seats into the coif. This is the FIRST METAL HEADPIECE on the unit — read it as the introduction of a metal helm tier. The skullcap is small and low-profile (not a full helm) — a separate full sallet helm arrives at this level. (2) SINGLE STEEL PAULDRON CAP ON THE ANATOMICAL-RIGHT SHOULDER — add a small rounded steel cap covering only the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder (the mace-arm side, viewer-LEFT side of frame). The pauldron is approximately 4 inches in diameter, cool silver-gray polished steel, slight domed curve, with three visible dark-iron rivets along its lower seam where it attaches to a thin warm-brown leather strap that runs across the chest underneath the existing livery tunic (the leather strap is mostly hidden under the tunic but visible briefly at each shoulder seam). The pauldron sits ON TOP OF the existing quilted leather short sleeve, partially covering the shoulder portion of that sleeve. The anatomical-LEFT shoulder (shield-arm side, viewer-RIGHT) remains UNCOVERED — no pauldron there at this level. (3) LEATHER GRIP CORD WRAPPING THE UPPER THIRD OF THE MACE HAFT — add a diagonal warm-brown leather grip cord wrapping the upper third of the wooden mace haft (the section just below the bulbous mace head where the hand currently grips). The cord is roughly half an inch wide, matte warm-brown leather, wrapped diagonally in a tight spiral pattern, secured with a small dark-iron pin at each end of the wrap. The wood-brown haft is still visible at the lower two-thirds of the mace; only the upper third is wrapped. (4) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS RADIATING FROM THE SHIELD BOSS — add four short flat iron strap-bands on the wooden face of the round shield, each band radiating outward from the central steel boss to the rim like the four arms of a cross (one band points up, one down, one to the viewer-left, one to the viewer-right). Each band is approximately 1 inch wide, dark cool-gray iron, riveted at three points along its length, ends just shy of the wooden shield rim. The wooden plank face of the shield remains visible BETWEEN the four cross arms; remains; the central steel boss remains. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression with no smile and no snarl; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris and visible whites of the eyes; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin tone with a soft rosy blush on the cheeks above the beard line; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering the chin and lower cheeks reaching the upper neck — trimmed short on the cheeks, NOT bushy, NOT wild, NOT long, NOT extended; his dark mustache continuous with the beard, covering the upper lip but not extending past the corners of the mouth; his dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering the entire scalp, ears, and neck down to the collarbone, with chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel painted with soft top-down highlights and warm-brown shadows under each scale's lower edge, draping around the shoulders to form a broad cowl-like collar; the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT half of the tunic chest is solid crimson-red, viewer-RIGHT half is mustard-yellow with a vertical crimson-red center stripe running collar to hem, simple round neckline, hem reaches mid-thigh — this red+mustard split livery tunic remains visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible; the existing brown leather forearm bracers wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms with a single visible cross-strap binding per bracer; the existing quilted brown leather short sleeves on both upper arms (per CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION above); the existing wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle (per CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION above); the existing brown leather hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip with flap closure and brass stud (per CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION above); the spiked wooden mace held in the anatomical-RIGHT hand — heavy warm-brown wooden haft, bulbous wooden mace head at the striking end painted slightly darker brown than the haft, six visible iron spikes (pyramidal cool-gray steel cones) protruding radially from the mace head with a single spike on the top apex, mace head dangling at hip height angled down toward the viewer-LEFT (the new leather grip cord wraps the upper third of the haft per Edit Goal item 2; the wooden bulb head shape and the six steel spikes remain unchanged); the round wooden buckler-style shield held in the anatomical-LEFT arm — approximately 24 inches in diameter, warm-brown wooden plank face with faint vertical wood grain, central cool-gray steel boss as a small dome, the wooden plank rim with no rivets; the dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff with soft painted vertical fold lines; the chunky warm-brown leather boots reaching mid-calf with a darker folded ankle cuff at the top and wide flat soles. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) is extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent at roughly 120 degrees. The hand grips the upper haft of the spiked wooden mace just below the mace head; the mace head hangs at hip height angled down toward the viewer-LEFT; the knuckles of the gripping hand are visible to camera. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) is bent at the elbow, with the forearm angled across the body bringing the round wooden shield up to upper-chest / shoulder height. The shield's flat face presents toward the viewer at a slight three-quarter angle, NOT perfectly flat to camera, NOT edge-on. The hand and forearm are concealed BEHIND the shield. The pose reads as a calm guard stance: mace ready but not raised, shield up but not braced for impact. The pose is NOT a swing, NOT a charge, NOT a block in motion — both arms are static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall) and approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide, including the mace and shield extending the silhouette envelope). TOP of mail coif at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas (~170px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at approximately 16.6% from bottom of canvas (~170px below). Character vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. Approximately 18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, approximately 20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the head or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT revert any feature: DO NOT remove the quilted leather short sleeves and re-expose bare biceps; DO NOT replace the leather belt with a rope cord; DO NOT remove the hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, or embossed dots to the shield rim or face — the wooden plank rim and face stay rivetless at this level (the continuous iron rim band arrives at this level); DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the mustard-yellow and crimson-red split livery tunic — the red+mustard chest livery must remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest; DO NOT replace the tunic with a body coat, gambeson, surcoat, or armor; DO NOT cover the tunic chest with scale mail, chain mail, or any metal armor at this level; DO NOT add scale mail, chain mail, breastplate, cuirass, or any body metal armor — the only metal additions at are the single steel pauldron cap and the four iron shield strap-bands; DO NOT add a full sallet, kettle helm, closed visor, or any structural helm beyond the new low-profile steel skullcap — the existing head addition is ONLY the steel skullcap rim visible under the mail coif (a full sallet helm arrives at this level); DO NOT remove or replace the mail coif — the coif still wraps the head over the new skullcap; DO NOT make the skullcap a leather forehead band — it MUST be polished cool silver-gray steel; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, scabbard, or any second belt accessory — the existing hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip is the ONLY belt accessory at this level; DO NOT add a second pouch on the anatomical-LEFT hip; DO NOT add a second pauldron on the anatomical-LEFT shoulder (shield-arm side) — exactly ONE pauldron at this level, on the mace-arm side only; DO NOT make the pauldron a layered multi-disc shape — it is a single rounded steel cap at this level (layered pauldrons arrive at this level); DO NOT add scale-mail short sleeves under the pauldron — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add steel pauldron straps as fully visible chest harness — the supporting leather strap is mostly hidden under the tunic; DO NOT add iron banding rings to the wooden mace head — those arrive at this level; DO NOT replace the wooden mace head with a flanged steel head — that arrives at this level; DO NOT change the iron spike count or arrangement on the mace head; DO NOT add a continuous iron rim band around the shield perimeter — that arrives at this level (only the new four iron cross strap-bands at this level (the shield rim stays plain wood until the existing continuous iron rim band)); DO NOT add embossed boss, sun-burst pattern, or steel-faced shield — those arrive at this level /; DO NOT replace the wooden shield face with metal; DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, square shield, or any non-round shape; DO NOT add painted heraldry, emblems, or coats of arms to the shield face; DO NOT add a second hip pouch — exactly ONE pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip; DO NOT add a second knife sheath — exactly ONE sheath on the anatomical-LEFT hip; DO NOT add the knife sheath on the same side as the existing pouch; DO NOT make the knife handle gold, jeweled, or ornate — plain bone-handle only; DO NOT add a leather X-strap chest harness, baldric, sword belt, or any additional chest strap (the only chest crossing element is the thin pauldron-supporting leather strap mostly hidden under the tunic); DO NOT add any bevor / steel collar / steel throat plate — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add steel vambraces or replace the leather bracers — those arrive at this level; DO NOT add steel sabatons, greaves, leg armor, or any leg metal at this level; DO NOT add gold trim, gold buckle, gold accents, gold rivets, gold band, or any gold anywhere; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT change any facial feature including the jawline, brow shape, eye color, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, mustache shape, beard shape, beard length, or skin tone; DO NOT grow the beard into a wild bushy beard or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard or trim it to clean-shaven; DO NOT change the hair color or hair fringe under the coif; DO NOT change the livery tunic colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color (dark blue / muted steel-blue) or boot color (warm brown); DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, add a ground plane, or add cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white — the background must be clean opaque white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
